zh.lifeSummary: Phillippus Charles Karl "Phillip" Maurer or Mason是John Mason的兒子，於1710年出生在德國。他於1730年之前與Anna Margaretha Laurenty在德國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1755年住在美國西維吉尼亞州哈普爾。他去世於1791年3月22日在美國賓夕法尼亞州法也特赫爾曼 堂史普享年81歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Phillippus Charles Karl "Phillip" Maurer or Mason是John Mason的儿子，于1710年出生在德国。他于1730年之前与Anna Margaretha Laurenty在德国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1755年住在美国西弗吉尼亚州哈普爾。他去世于1791年3月22日在美国宾夕法尼亚州法也特赫爾曼 堂史普享年81岁。

nameMeanings.name: Maurer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a mason, especially a builder of walls of stone or brick, from an agent derivative of Middle High German mūre, German Mauer ‘wall’ (from Latin murus ‘wall’, especially a city wall). In the Middle Ages the majority of dwellings were built of wood (or lath and plaster), and this term would have specifically denoted someone employed in building defensive walls, castles, churches, and other public buildings. This form of the surname is also found in some other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), the Netherlands, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and Slovenia (see also 2 below). Compare Mourer and Mowrer .

In some cases also an Americanized or Germanized form of Slovenian Mavrer or Mavrar: occupational name of German origin (see 1 above), at least in some cases applied as a translation into German of the Slovenian surname Zidar .

variants.name: Mowrer
meanings: Americanized form of German   Maurer  .

variants.name: Mourer
meanings: Americanized form of German   Maurer  .
French (Lorraine): probably of German origin, an altered form   Maurer   or   Murer  .

variants.name: Mauer
meanings: German: variant of   Maurer  . This surname is also found in some other European countries, e.g. in France (Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below).
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived by a wall, Middle High German mūre, German Mauer. As a Jewish name it can be artificial. Compare Mower .
Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian   Maver  . See also 1 above.

variants.name: Meurer
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Maurer  .

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Mason
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English masoun ‘mason, stone worker, builder in stone’ (Old Central French maçon, masson). A Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun) gives rise to Machin and its variants. See also Mayson . Stonemasonry was a hugely important craft in the Middle Ages.
Italian (Veneto): variant of   Masone  .
Altered form of French   Masson  .

variants.name: Masson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Lucien, Alain, Henri, Pierre, Yves, Agathe, Aime, Cecile, Clovis, Francois, Francoise.
Scottish: variant of   Mason   ‘mason’.
Scottish: variant of   Meston  .

variants.name: Machin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Juan, Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Mario, Miguel, Pedro, Blanca, Diego, Luis, Ramon.
English: variant of   Machen  . This is the more common form of the name in Britain and Ireland.
Spanish (Machín): probably a nickname from machín ‘boor, lout’, often applied to a blacksmith's apprentice.

variants.name: Macon
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places so called, e.g. in Saône-et-Loire.
French: from the old oblique form of the ancient Germanic personal name Mako, a variant of Mago, a short form of compound names formed with maht ‘strength, power’ as the first element.
French (Maçon): occupational name for a mason, (Old) French maçon.

variants.name: Machen
meanings: English: variant of Mason , from the Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun).

variants.name: Meason
meanings: English: variant of   Mason  .

nameMeanings.name: Phillippus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish and Dutch: variant of   Phillips  .

variants.name: Phillips
meanings: Variant spelling of Philip , in part a reflection of the surname, which is usually spelled Phillips.

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

nameMeanings.name: Karl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of   Charles  , also used in the English-speaking world.  See also  Carl .

variants.name: Charles
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

variants.name: Carl
meanings: From an old-fashioned German spelling variant of Karl , the German version of Charles . It is now increasingly used in the English-speaking world, and for some reason is particularly popular in Wales.

nameMeanings.name: "Phillip"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Philip , in part a reflection of the surname, which is usually spelled Phillips.

en.lifeSummary: Phillippus Charles Karl "Phillip" Maurer or Mason was born in 1710, in Germany as the son of John Mason. He married Anna Margaretha Laurenty before 1730, in Germany. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Hampshire, West Virginia, United States in 1755. He died on 22 March 1791, in German Township, Fayette, Pennsylvania, United States, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Phillippus Charles Karl "Phillip" Maurer or Mason est né en 1710, à Allemagne, fils de John Mason. Il a épousé Anna Margaretha Laurenty avant 1730, à Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il a vécu à Hampshire, Virginie occidentale, États-Unis en 1755. Il est décédé le 22 mars 1791, à German Township, Fayette, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 81 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Phillippus Charles Karl "Phillip" Maurer or Mason wurde 1710 in Deutschland, als Sohn von John Mason geboren. Er hat vor 1730 in Deutschland Anna Margaretha Laurenty geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1755 hat er in Hampshire, West Virginia, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb am 22. März 1791 im Alter von 81 Jahren in German Township, Fayette, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: Phillippus Charles Karl "Phillip" Maurer or Masonは1710年にドイツでJohn Masonの息子として生まれた。1730年以前にドイツでAnna Margaretha Laurentyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1755年にはアメリカ合衆国ウエストバージニア州ハンプシュアイアに住んでいた。彼は1791年3月22日にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州ファイエットジャーマン トウンシュイップで81歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Phillippus Charles Karl "Phillip" Maurer or Mason(은)는 1710년 독일에서 John Mason의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1730년 이전 독일에서 Anna Margaretha Laurenty(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1755년도에 미국 웨스트버지니아 주 함프실르에서 살았다. 그는 1791년 3월 22일 미국 펜실베이니아 주 파예트 그먼 타운시프에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Phillippus Charles Karl "Phillip" Maurer or Mason nasceu em 1710, em Alemanha filho de John Mason. Ele casou-se com Anna Margaretha Laurenty antes 1730, em Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele viveu em Hampshire, Virgínia Ocidental, Estados Unidos da América em 1755. Ele faleceu em 22 de março de 1791, em German Township, Fayette, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América, com 81 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Phillippus Charles Karl "Phillip" Maurer or Mason родился в 1710 г. в местности Германия. Его отца звали John Mason. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna Margaretha Laurenty перед 1730 г. в местности Германия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности хампшир, Западная Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в 1755 году. Он умер 22 марта 1791 г. в местности джирман тоуншип, феиетт, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 81 год.
